hero.title: Mimamsa School of Philosophy
hero.subtitle: The science of interpretation and ritual knowledge

introduction.title: About Mimamsa
introduction.content: Mimamsa, meaning 'investigation' or 'examination', is one of the six classical schools of Indian philosophy. Founded by Jaimini, it focuses on the interpretation of Vedic texts and the performance of rituals. Mimamsa provides a systematic approach to understanding dharma (duty) and the proper conduct of religious ceremonies.

history.title: Historical Development
history.content: Mimamsa developed as a response to the need for systematic interpretation of Vedic texts. The school was formalized by Jaimini in his Mimamsa Sutras around 200 BCE. Later commentators like Sabara, Kumarila Bhatta, and Prabhakara further developed the school's philosophical foundations.

timeline.event: Sabara writes his commentary on Mimamsa Sutras
Kumarila Bhatta and Prabhakara develop different schools within Mimamsa

principles.title: Dharma as Central
principles.description: Dharma is the central concept in Mimamsa, referring to the duties and obligations that maintain cosmic order
principles.details: Mimamsa views dharma as the foundation of all human action and the key to achieving the highest good.

principles.title: Vedic Authority
principles.description: The Vedas are considered the ultimate source of knowledge and authority
principles.details: Mimamsa argues that the Vedas are eternal, authorless, and infallible sources of knowledge about dharma.

principles.title: Ritual Performance
principles.description: Proper performance of rituals is essential for maintaining cosmic order
principles.details: Mimamsa provides detailed guidelines for the correct performance of Vedic rituals and their significance.

principles.title: Karma Theory
principles.description: Actions have consequences that affect future experiences
principles.details: Mimamsa develops a sophisticated theory of karma that explains how actions create results in this life and beyond.

texts.title: Mimamsa Sutras
texts.author: Jaimini
texts.description: The foundational text of Mimamsa philosophy, covering the interpretation of Vedic texts and ritual performance
texts.significance: Establishes the basic principles and methodology of Mimamsa

texts.title: Sabara Bhashya
texts.author: Sabara
texts.description: The earliest surviving commentary on Mimamsa Sutras
texts.significance: Provides detailed explanations of Jaimini's sutras and establishes many key concepts

texts.title: Works of Kumarila Bhatta
texts.author: Kumarila Bhatta
texts.description: Extensive commentaries and independent works on Mimamsa
texts.significance: Develops the Bhatta school of Mimamsa with emphasis on the reality of the external world

practicalApplications.title: Practical Applications
practicalApplications.subtitle: How Mimamsa principles apply to modern life

applications.title: Ethical Framework
applications.description: Mimamsa provides a systematic approach to understanding duty and ethical obligations
applications.modernRelevance: Helps in developing a structured approach to moral decision-making and understanding one's responsibilities

applications.title: Text Interpretation
applications.description: Mimamsa's methods of textual analysis can be applied to understanding any authoritative text
applications.modernRelevance: Useful for legal interpretation, religious studies, and academic research

applications.title: Ritual and Ceremony
applications.description: Understanding the significance and proper performance of rituals and ceremonies
applications.modernRelevance: Applies to religious practices, cultural ceremonies, and formal procedures

courses.title: Mimamsa Courses
courses.subtitle: Learn Mimamsa philosophy through structured courses

availableCourses.title: Introduction to Mimamsa
availableCourses.description: Learn the fundamental concepts of Mimamsa philosophy and its approach to Vedic interpretation

availableCourses.title: Advanced Mimamsa
availableCourses.description: Deep dive into Mimamsa epistemology, metaphysics, and its relationship with other schools

availableCourses.title: Mimamsa Text Study
availableCourses.description: Detailed study of key Mimamsa texts with Sanskrit reading and interpretation

resources.title: Recommended Books
items: Introduction to Mimamsa by Ganganatha Jha
Mimamsa Philosophy by P. V. Kane
